title: festuca ovina subvar. cinerea
intro: Festuca ovina subvar. cinerea, commonly known as Sheep's Fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and it is said to have various health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating wounds and skin conditions. Sheep's Fescue is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used in folk remedies to treat respiratory issues and digestive problems. Additionally, the plant is rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which are believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine.
